steps.title: Support the both-bacteria claim
steps.content: Serrano and colleagues concluded that both A. ratkowskyi and A. boritolerans might have played a role in the development of multicellular organisms. The strongest support would show that both bacterial exposures led S. rosetta to form colonies.

steps.title: Reject one-sided findings
steps.content: Choice B supports only Harper's bacterium and not Nair's. Choice C supports only Nair's bacterium and not Harper's. Choice D says neither exposure produced colony formation, directly weakening the conclusion about both bacteria. The answer is A.
